nodes.title: Validate storage contract first
nodes.why: Failing early gives a clear error when the caller passes the wrong object.
nodes.alternative: Assume storage is valid and let calls fail later.
nodes.tradeoff: One guard adds a tiny cost but prevents confusing runtime failures.

nodes.title: Store absolute expiration time
nodes.why: Using an absolute timestamp makes read-time expiration checks simple.
nodes.alternative: Store only ttlMs and recompute from write time metadata elsewhere.
nodes.tradeoff: Absolute exp is easy to compare, but depends on current clock behavior.

nodes.title: Wrap value in a JSON envelope
nodes.why: Envelope format lets us store both value and metadata in one storage entry.
nodes.alternative: Use separate keys for value and expiry.
nodes.tradeoff: Single-key payload is compact and atomic, but requires JSON parse on reads.

nodes.title: Return null for missing keys
nodes.why: No stored data should be a clean cache miss, not an exception.
nodes.tradeoff: Null-miss is easy for callers, but they must explicitly handle null.

nodes.title: Check parsed payload shape
nodes.why: This avoids trusting malformed payloads that do not match expected format.
nodes.alternative: Assume parse success always means valid payload.
nodes.tradeoff: Extra validation branch for safer behavior on legacy/corrupt data.

nodes.title: Delete expired entries on read
nodes.why: Removing stale data keeps storage clean and prevents repeated expired reads.
nodes.alternative: Return null but leave expired item in storage.
nodes.tradeoff: Cleanup adds a write on expired reads, but avoids storage buildup.

nodes.title: Recover from bad JSON by removing it
nodes.why: Corrupted entries should not keep breaking reads, so we drop them immediately.
nodes.alternative: Swallow parse errors and keep broken data.
nodes.tradeoff: Aggressive cleanup may drop data, but prevents repeated parse failures.

nodes.title: Expose manual remove
nodes.why: TTL handles expiry, but callers still need explicit invalidation when data changes.
nodes.alternative: Rely only on expiry and do not expose remove.
nodes.tradeoff: One extra API method, but better control for cache invalidation flows.
